mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as market participants price in continued economic expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending generally supports equity markets.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 are negative in more than 40% of periods where RSAFS growth is positive, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold indicates tightening labor markets, signaling a strong economy that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low unemployment is historically correlated with economic expansion.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE falls below 5.0%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and CPI inflation is accelerating, it signals an accommodative monetary policy in an inflationary environment, which can drive investors towards equities as a hedge against inflation and due to lower real discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and rising inflation can favor equities.
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 2.0%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are predominantly negative or show no significant positive trend.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic recovery, a surge in housing starts indicates robust investment and consumer confidence, which acts as a leading indicator for broader economic strength, translating into short-term positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a key indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If, during a declared post-recession recovery period, a 10% 3-month average growth in HOUST does not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing and supply-side capacity, indicating a healthy and growing economy that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production growth is a sign of economic strength.
failure_condition: If, after INDPRO shows 3 consecutive months of growth exceeding 0.5% per month, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a fundamental driver of economic activity.
regime_hint: any regime with positive job growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) are accelerating, it signifies broad-based strength in aggregate demand, which quickly translates into positive market sentiment and short-term S&P 500 gains over 5 trading days, as investors anticipate strong corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 3.0% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 1.0% per month
evidence_refs: fact:Strong GDP and retail sales indicate robust demand.
failure_condition: If, during periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth conditions are met, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative on average or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, persistent high inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real value of cash and fixed-income assets, pushing investors into equities as a store of value, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Equities can be an inflation hedge in low-rate environments.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or show no statistically significant positive trend.
